status.phase: Beta release 0.1.1 published; community submission preparation
status.summary: GitHub beta release 0.1.1 is published with release assets main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css. 0.1.1 includes author metadata and keeps plugin id german-law-lookup. Main additionally contains README privacy/network access documentation for community-submission readiness. Runtime behavior remains unchanged: no parser runtime, provider, cache, UI, or insertion behavior changes beyond prior test coverage.

blockers.title: Primary law data source not yet validated
blockers.notes: Closed after validation. Gesetze im Internet HTML is already runtime-validated as the official federal-law provider path. Technical XML access was validated on 2026-05-22: gii-toc.xml returned 200 application/xml, and bgb/xml.zip, stgb/xml.zip, and gg/xml.zip returned 200 application/zip with GiI norm XML files. XML extractor implementation remains optional future robustness work, not an MVP blocker.

blockers.title: NeuRIS API stability and coverage must be verified
blockers.notes: Deferred, not MVP-blocking. NeuRIS remains the strategic future provider target, but official public information still describes the portal/API as a test-phase/development service with restricted and gradually expanding data coverage. The Stable MVP already uses runtime-validated official Gesetze im Internet HTML, and GiI XML access has been technically validated. NeuRIS must not replace or destabilize the current GiI provider path until API stability, schema, and law coverage are revalidated against the final or sufficiently stable portal.

issues.title: Citation and retrieval metadata required
issues.notes: Every inserted law text must include source, law code, section and retrieval date.

issues.title: Scope must remain Bundesrecht-only for MVP
issues.notes: No Landesrecht, EU law, case law or commentary in MVP.

issues: Additional official English law text sources beyond BGB, GG, StGB, OWiG, HGB, GmbHG, AktG, InsO, AufenthG, FamFG, StAG, PAuswG, AGG, GWB, SGB XIV, ZPO, StPO, VwGO, BDSG, UWG, VVG, BVerfGG, and pure EGBGB articles remain future work.
SGB V English text remains intentionally unconfigured for general lookup because the verified official English HTML is partial and starts at Section 64e, not Section 1.

tasks.title: Create Obsidian plugin scaffold
tasks.notes: Plugin scaffold exists and Stable MVP is runtime-validated.

tasks.title: Implement legal citation parser
tasks.notes: Legal citation parser is implemented and covers supported §, article, SGB, EGBGB, GG, and FreizügG/EU forms.

tasks.title: Implement mock law source
Insert formatted law text into active note
tasks.notes: Formatted law text insertion into the active Obsidian note is implemented and runtime-validated.

tasks.title: Implement provider registry
Implement mock API provider before real network providers
Validate NeuRIS API endpoints and schema
tasks.notes: Deferred after provider assessment on 2026-05-22. NeuRIS is strategically relevant but remains in test/development status with restricted and evolving coverage according to official public project information. No runtime/provider code should depend on NeuRIS until API stability, schema, coverage, and fallback behavior are revalidated. Current Stable MVP remains anchored on verified Gesetze im Internet official-de/translation-en paths.

tasks.title: Validate Gesetze im Internet XML fallback access
tasks.notes: Validated Gesetze im Internet XML fallback access. gii-toc.xml returned 200 application/xml. bgb/xml.zip, stgb/xml.zip, and gg/xml.zip returned 200 application/zip. BGB xml.zip contained BJNR001950896.xml with GiI norm DTD, BGB metadata, builddate, title, and status/change metadata. No runtime/provider code was changed.

tasks.title: Implement GesetzeImInternetProvider fixture fallback for BGB § 823
tasks.notes: Added fixture-tested HTML fallback for BGB § 823 with official-source/non-authoritative metadata.

tasks.title: Update provider order to NeuRIS, Gesetze im Internet, Mock
Handle Gesetze im Internet 404 as unresolved null
Handle non-404 Gesetze im Internet failures as LawProviderUnavailableError
Normalize Gesetze im Internet letter suffix URLs to lowercase
Live-validate full provider chain for BGB § 823
tasks.notes: Reference: BGB § 823. Provider call order: neuris -> gesetze-im-internet. Final providerId: gesetze-im-internet. MockLawProvider called: false. LawProviderUnavailableError thrown: false. sourceUrl: https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/bgb/__823.html. Result: resolved via Gesetze im Internet fallback.

tasks.title: Expand Gesetze im Internet law-code support beyond BGB
tasks.notes: Expanded the explicit supportedLaws mapping only for live-verified Gesetze-im-Internet section URLs that fit the current parser and provider model. Newly added mappings in this expansion: AO -> ao_1977, AktG -> aktg, ArbGG -> arbgg, AsylG -> asylvfg_1992, AufenthG -> aufenthg_2004, BetrVG -> betrvg, BUrlG -> burlg, EStG -> estg, FamFG -> famfg, FGO -> fgo, GewStG -> gewstg, GmbHG -> gmbhg, GVG -> gvg, GwG -> gwg_2017, HGB -> hgb, InsO -> inso, JGG -> jgg, KAGB -> kagb, KSchG -> kschg, KStG -> kstg_1977, PAuswG -> pauswg, SGG -> sgg, StAG -> stag, StPO -> stpo, TzBfG -> tzbfg, UmwG -> umwg_1995, UStG -> ustg_1980, VwGO -> vwgo, VwVfG -> vwvfg, WpHG -> wphg, ZPO -> zpo, ZVG -> zvg. Existing BGB and StGB support remained intact. URL helper tests cover every added mapping. Fixture-backed provider tests cover representative added laws including HGB § 1, ZPO § 1, VwVfG § 1, StAG § 1, KAGB § 1, and GwG § 10. Parser, provider fallback, requestUrl transport, cache keys, cache live-first behavior, metadata-footer toggle behavior, and terminal provider-failure semantics remain unchanged. Manual Obsidian runtime validation confirmed the expanded explicit mapping set works. Representative tested laws resolved and inserted successfully; unsupported/skipped candidates remain intentionally outside current support.

tasks.title: Investigate skipped Gesetze-im-Internet candidates with uncertain paths or unsupported reference forms
tasks.notes: Completed/obsolete follow-up task. Previously skipped candidates EGBGB, OWiG, KWG, FreizügG/EU, and SGB I/II/V/X class issues were resolved by later dedicated parser/provider work. Remaining future work must be tracked as new, source-verified law-specific tasks only.

tasks.title: Implement cache layer for retrieved law sections
tasks.notes: Local LawSectionCache implemented. Cache key format is LAWCODE:section. lawCode is uppercased for the key; section is trimmed and lowercased for the key only, so letter-suffixed sections such as 312G and 312g resolve to the same cache key. Live provider chain remains authoritative and is called first. Successful live LawSection results are cached best-effort; cache write failures do not fail the lookup. Cache fallback is only checked after live providers return null. Provider failures remain terminal and must not be hidden by cache. null/not-found results are not cached. Cached mock-provider entries are ignored unless MockLawProvider is explicitly enabled. Tests cover cache key normalization, live-first behavior, provider failure behavior, best-effort cache writes, and mock-cache isolation.

tasks.title: Disable MockLawProvider outside development/test mode
tasks.notes: Default provider order is now: 1. neuris, 2. gesetze-im-internet, 3. no mock. MockLawProvider can only be enabled explicitly with enableMockLawProvider: true. ProviderRegistry behavior remains unchanged: null falls through and LawProviderUnavailableError remains terminal. Plugin startup still performs no law-provider network calls.

tasks.title: Implement note insertion UX for formatted law text
tasks.notes: After successful lookup, the modal shows 'In aktuelle Note einfügen'. Insertion uses CitationFormatter markdown and happens only after explicit user click. The formatted law section is inserted at the current cursor position in the active Markdown editor. If no active Markdown editor exists, the plugin shows an Obsidian Notice. Stale lookup protection remains preserved.

tasks.title: Fix Obsidian runtime transport for external law-provider HTTP
tasks.notes: Global fetch worked in Node validation but failed in Obsidian runtime for Gesetze im Internet. Runtime providers now use Obsidian requestUrl transport. Constructor injection remains available for fixture tests, and startup still performs no law-provider network calls.

tasks.title: Clean Gesetze im Internet section extraction for BGB § 823
tasks.notes: Obsidian runtime validation confirmed BGB § 823 resolves through Gesetze im Internet. Extraction now prefers norm paragraph blocks and excludes footer/navigation artifacts. Confirmed removed artifacts: zum Seitenanfang, Impressum, Datenschutz, Barrierefreiheitserklärung, Feedback-Formular, Seite ausdrucken. Legal paragraphs (1) and (2) remain present. The insert button appears after successful lookup.

tasks.title: Render lookup result preview instead of raw Markdown
tasks.notes: The lookup modal result area now renders a readable DOM preview after successful lookup instead of showing raw Markdown text. The preview shows the section heading/title, readable law text paragraphs, and the existing source/cache metadata only when the modal toggle is enabled. Toggling "Quellen- und Cache-Hinweis einfügen" updates the preview immediately. Insert behavior remains unchanged: the inserted note content is still generated by CitationFormatter, and stale lookup protection, provider semantics, requestUrl transport, cache behavior, and metadata-footer ownership remain unchanged. Manual Obsidian runtime validation confirmed that the modal shows a readable rendered preview, that the source/cache metadata appears and disappears immediately with the existing toggle, and that insertion still works through the existing CitationFormatter markdown output.

tasks.title: Manually validate insertion into an active Obsidian note
tasks.notes: Obsidian runtime plugin test succeeded. Lookup flow, visible insertion action, and insertion into an active note were manually validated after the cache layer and parser improvements.

tasks.title: Improve law-reference input placeholder and section-first parser coverage
tasks.notes: Placeholder changed to "z. B. § 823 BGB". Parser now accepts section-first input without §, including "823 BGB", "823a BGB", "312g BGB", and "312G BGB". Existing supported forms remain supported: "§ 823 BGB", "BGB 823", "BGB § 823", "KAGB § 1", and "GwG 10". No provider, registry, or transport behavior changed.

tasks.title: Add minimal local cache settings
tasks.notes: Added settings for enableLawSectionCache, default true, and lawSectionCacheTtlDays, default null. The settings UI can disable the cache wrapper or set a positive TTL in days. Cache settings preserve live-first provider semantics: live providers are still called first, cache fallback is only checked after live providers return null, provider failures remain terminal, null/not-found results are not cached, and cached mock-provider entries remain gated by explicit MockLawProvider opt-in.

tasks.title: Add lookup-modal toggle for inserted source/cache metadata footer
tasks.notes: Runtime validation succeeded. The lookup modal toggle "Quellen- und Cache-Hinweis einfügen" is visible, persists its selected state, and controls whether inserted Markdown includes the Quelle/Cache footer. With the toggle enabled, the existing metadata footer is inserted. With the toggle disabled, the inserted law text keeps the heading and quoted section text but omits the footer. Lookup and insertion behavior remain functional.

tasks.title: Record additional runtime law-code coverage observations
tasks.notes: Manual Obsidian runtime testing after the metadata footer toggle confirmed additional successful law-code lookups for KAGB and STAG. StGB lookup did not work and should be investigated separately before expanding Gesetze im Internet coverage.

tasks.title: Investigate failed StGB lookup
tasks.notes: Root cause: StGB already parsed correctly through the generic uppercasing parser path, but GesetzeImInternetProvider did not support STGB in supportedLaws, so no Gesetze im Internet URL was generated and the provider returned null without fetching. Added parser coverage for StGB input variants, added STGB -> stgb / Strafgesetzbuch mapping, and added provider tests for https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/stgb/__242.html while preserving provider fallback, 404/null, non-404/LawProviderUnavailableError, transport, cache, and UI invariants. Subsequent Obsidian runtime validation confirmed that StGB lookup, insertion, and the metadata toggle now work end to end.

tasks.title: Normalize canonical display abbreviation for StGB output
tasks.notes: Root cause: internal lookup code remains uppercase by parser and lookup design, but the mapped LawSection reused STGB for display, so inserted output showed '§ 242 STGB' instead of the canonical legal abbreviation '§ 242 StGB'. Initial live-result mapping fix corrected fresh GesetzeImInternetProvider results, but review identified a P2 upgrade-path gap: pre-existing cached STGB LawSection entries could still surface uppercase STGB during cache fallback because CitationFormatter renders section.lawCode verbatim. The fix adds a small shared canonical display-law-code helper, keeps parser normalization, provider lookup keys, cache keys, requestUrl transport, provider fallback semantics, and metadata-footer toggle behavior unchanged, and normalizes cached pre-existing STGB entries on fallback/read before returning the cached LawSection. Runtime validation confirmed that StGB § 242 now displays and inserts as "§ 242 StGB – Diebstahl" rather than "§ 242 STGB – Diebstahl".

tasks.title: Polish lookup-modal preview and action layout
tasks.notes: Visual/UI-only follow-up after the rendered preview implementation. The modal title remains "Deutsches Gesetz nachschlagen". The preview block spacing was increased, the result container was made more readable, the metadata toggle was visually de-emphasized relative to the preview, and the "In aktuelle Note einfügen" action remains easy to find. Toggle-on/off preview updates, CitationFormatter insertion output, stale lookup protection, parser/provider/cache behavior, and lifecycle semantics remain unchanged.

tasks.title: Add explicit GG article-reference support
tasks.notes: Added explicit article-reference parsing only for GG inputs: "Art. 1 GG", "Artikel 1 GG", "GG Art. 1", "GG Artikel 1", and compatibility form "Art 1 GG". Internal references now carry a minimal referenceType field so GG article references remain distinct from existing section references. GesetzeImInternetProvider now supports GG with verified official path pattern https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/gg/art_1.html and title "Grundgesetz für die Bundesrepublik Deutschland", while existing section-style URLs such as BGB § 823 -> /bgb/__823.html and StGB § 242 -> /stgb/__242.html remain unchanged. CitationFormatter, preview rendering, and cache fallback now display Art. for GG article references; cache keys remain deterministic and distinguish article vs. section references without invalidating existing section-style cache entries. ProviderRegistry semantics, Obsidian requestUrl transport, metadata toggle behavior, preview layout, live-first cache semantics, and terminal provider-failure behavior remain unchanged. Manual Obsidian runtime validation confirmed that GG article references work end to end: Art. 1 GG / Artikel 1 GG / GG Art. 1 resolve through Gesetze im Internet, preview and inserted Markdown use Art. labels instead of § labels, and existing § lookups remain functional.

tasks.title: Add supported-law diagnostics view
tasks.notes: Added a settings/UI-only diagnostics view named "Unterstützte Gesetze" that renders the explicitly supported Gesetze-im-Internet mappings from shared mapping metadata, grouped into paragraph and article references with German labels, titles, reference types, and example inputs. The settings view performs no network calls and does not probe provider URLs dynamically. Provider behavior, parser behavior, requestUrl transport, cache keys, cache fallback semantics, preview rendering, lookup-modal behavior, and CitationFormatter insertion output remain unchanged.

tasks.title: Improve supported-law diagnostics example-input UX
tasks.notes: Updated the settings/diagnostics metadata so each explicitly supported law now exposes multiple supported input forms in a stable order, with the first example using natural citation order such as "§ 823 BGB" and "Art. 1 GG". The settings UI renders these compactly without adding any runtime provider calls. Parser behavior, provider mappings, requestUrl transport, cache behavior, lookup flow, preview rendering, and CitationFormatter insertion output remain unchanged.

tasks.title: Improve supported-law diagnostics example layout
tasks.notes: Updated the settings/UI-only diagnostics view to render each supported input form as a compact wrapped chip instead of one joined text string. This improves readability in narrow settings panels while preserving the existing exampleInputs metadata and ordering. Parser behavior, provider behavior, requestUrl transport, cache behavior, lookup flow, preview rendering, and CitationFormatter insertion output remain unchanged.

tasks.title: Evaluate Gesetze im Internet XML extractor as optional robustness path
tasks.notes: Future optional task. XML access is validated, but the Stable MVP already works through official GiI HTML. Any XML extractor must preserve parser behavior, provider fallback semantics, variant-aware cache keys, preview/insertion output, and no-translation invariants.

tasks.title: Revisit NeuRIS provider after API stabilization
tasks.notes: Future strategic task. Re-check NeuRIS only after the Rechtsinformationsportal/API is final or sufficiently stable. Verification must cover endpoint availability, schema, law coverage for currently supported lookups, metadata quality, version/fassung behavior, error semantics, and compatibility with existing provider fallback/cache/UI invariants.

mvp_flow: Plugin inserts text into active note
invariants: Plugin must not modify notes without explicit user action
Inserted law text may omit the source/cache footer only when the user explicitly disables it in the lookup modal
MVP must only handle German federal law
Fetching logic must remain separate from UI logic

decisions.title: Use Codex for repository edits and ChatGPT for architecture/debugging
Use API-first provider architecture
decisions.notes: The plugin should retrieve German law texts via APIs where possible. Provider abstraction is required to support NeuRIS, Gesetze im Internet fallback, and optional Open Legal Data.

decisions.title: Use Gesetze im Internet as fallback for confirmed NeuRIS coverage gaps
decisions.notes: Gesetze im Internet is used as the official/public consolidated-law fallback for confirmed NeuRIS coverage gaps, starting with BGB § 823.

decisions.title: Provider fallback lifecycle live-validated for BGB § 823
decisions.notes: NeuRIS coverage gaps may fall through to Gesetze im Internet when the first provider returns null, but provider failures must remain terminal.

decisions.title: MockLawProvider requires explicit development/demo opt-in
decisions.notes: MockLawProvider must not be registered in normal runtime. It is development/demo only and requires explicit opt-in.

decisions.title: Note insertion requires explicit user action
decisions.notes: Law text insertion must require explicit user action and must insert formatted source and citation metadata, not raw provider output.

decisions.title: Obsidian runtime law-provider HTTP uses requestUrl transport
decisions.notes: Law providers in Obsidian runtime must use Obsidian requestUrl transport, not global fetch, because browser/Electron transport behavior can fail for external law-provider websites even when Node validation succeeds.

decisions.title: Gesetze im Internet extraction prefers norm paragraph blocks
decisions.notes: Gesetze im Internet extraction must prefer actual norm paragraph blocks and exclude page chrome, navigation, footer, print links, and script/style/comment fragments from LawSection.text.

decisions.title: Do not add general SGB V English law text support
details: Verified official German SGB V § 1 path: sgb_5/__1.html.
Verified official English SGB V HTML path: englisch_sgb_5/englisch_sgb_5.html.
Did not add SGB V translation-en mapping because the official English HTML is partial and visibly starts at Section 64e, not Section 1.
SGB V § 1 with English toggle enabled therefore correctly falls back to official-de.
The plugin still never generates translations.
No code changes were made.
Validated git diff --check, npm test, and npm run build.
